commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves deprecated package `version.sha256hash` as the deterministic legacy `/download` ZIP hash while keeping canonical artifact hashes on `artifact.sha256`, updating scanners/exports/docs, and adding a guarded backfill plus tests.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a high-confidence path from the PR body: the listed legacy OpenClaw install command fails against the reported production fixture with an integrity mismatch. I did not rerun that production command during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A collaborator-authored PR with production hash semantics, a temporary backfill, and pending after-fix proof needs maintainer review rather than automated cleanup or repair.

target.title: fix: preserve legacy package ZIP hashes
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve deprecated `version.sha256hash` as the SHA-256 of the exact deterministic ZIP returned by legacy `/download`
- keep `artifact.sha256` canonical for the actual release artifact (`.tgz` for npm-pack, ZIP for legacy releases)
- stop VirusTotal scanning from overwriting the legacy compatibility hash
- add a temporary guarded, resumable, dry-run-first production backfill for existing npm-pack releases
- document the deprecated compatibility contract and update security/export consumers to use canonical artifact hashes
- repair an existing one-line test-helper lint failure that blocked the required static gate

## Migration Strategy

The temporary `packageLegacyZipHashBackfill` action is intentionally hand-rolled because it must read storage blobs and build deterministic ZIPs inside an action, which `@convex-dev/migrations` mutations cannot do directly.

Safety controls:

- dry run required before apply
- bounded cursor pagination (`5` default, `10` maximum)
- resumable cursor returned after every page
- explicit `BACKFILL_LEGACY_PACKAGE_ZIP_HASHES` apply token
- idempotent match handling and stale-write guard
- includes soft-deleted releases so restored legacy downloads remain compatible

After production apply and verification, the temporary action and its tests will be removed in a follow-up PR.

## Proof

Reported production fixture before the backfill:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<isolated-temp-state> \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<isolated-temp-config> \
npx -y openclaw@2026.4.9 plugins install 'clawhub:@openviking/openclaw-plugin@2026.5.21'
```

Exit code: `1`

```text
ClawHub archive integrity mismatch for "@openviking/openclaw-plugin@2026.5.21": expected sha256-ey+uw89qc3qkrhwsTpLnT9DE0ZaO1UnSKc8YPm7dUSo=, got sha256-4l5JyVzCRNTHh5HagoIGTjlRgKaj9TRnxJa60EIxubQ=.
```

Current production artifact hashes:

```text
version.sha256hash / npm .tgz: 7b2faec3cf6a737aa4ae1c2c4e92e74fd0c4d1968ed549d229cf183e6edd512a
legacy /download ZIP:          e25e49c95cc244d4c78791da8282064e395180a6a3f53467c496bad04231b9b4
```

The exact command will be rerun after deployment and backfill; success output will be posted as production proof.

## Validation

- `bunx vitest run convex/lib/packageArtifacts.test.ts convex/packageLegacyZipHashBackfill.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts convex/vt.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts scripts/security-dataset/convexExport.test.ts src/__tests__/plugin-security-audit-route.test.tsx --reporter=dot` (`561` passed)
- `bunx vitest run convex/publishers.test.ts --reporter=dot` (`70` passed)
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit` (`3155` passed, `1` skipped; coverage gate passed)
- `bun run ci:types-build`
- `bun run ci:packages`
- `bun run ci:e2e-http`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bunx convex codegen`

## Review Notes

- Manual branch review found and fixed three correctness issues: npm-pack canonical consumers no longer fall back to the legacy ZIP hash; soft-deleted/restorable releases are included; scan backfill no longer repeatedly selects releases solely because the legacy hash is absent.
- The automated Codex reviewer recursively invoked the autoreview helper instead of completing, so it was stopped as a tooling loop. No alternate reviewer executable is installed.
- `bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable` is blocked by an unrelated stale local `packageReleases.clawScanNote` fixture. The production deploy workflow must successfully validate and deploy Convex before the guarded production dry run.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
